Number of Arms Included.evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 1 states: 'Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel.' This indicates two treatment arms: one with darolutamide and one with placebo, both in combination with androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel.

Control Arm.evidence: On page 1, in the 'METHODS' section, it states: 'Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel.' It further clarifies: 'The darolutamide group had a significant reduction in the risk of death by 32.5% (HR, 0.68; 95% CI, 0.57 to 0.80; P<.0001) compared with the placebo group.' This indicates that the placebo group serves as the comparison group, i.e., the control arm.

Treatment Arm - N.evidence: On page 3, Figure 1 (CONSORT flow diagram) shows that 1,306 patients were randomly assigned. The diagram then branches to 'Darolutamide + docetaxel + ADT (n = 651)' and 'Placebo + docetaxel + ADT (n = 655)'. This indicates 651 participants were in the experimental arm. This is further confirmed in Table 1 on page 5, under 'All Patients (N = 1,305)', where the 'Darolutamide' column shows 'n = 651'.

Treatment Arm(s).value: Darolutamide + androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) + docetaxel; Placebo + androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) + docetaxel
Treatment Arm(s).evidence: The 'METHODS' section of the abstract states: 'Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel.' This is further supported in the 'Introduction' section on page 1 and 'Trial Design' on page 2, which specify the two arms as 'darolutamide or placebo, in combination with ADT and docetaxel'. Figure 1 also clearly labels the two arms as 'Darolutamide + docetaxel + ADT' and 'Placebo + docetaxel + ADT'.

Median Follow-Up Duration (mo).evidence: The document reports 'median survival' and 'median treatment duration' in various sections (e.g., page 4 under 'Primary End Point', page 11 under 'DISCUSSION'), but it does not explicitly state the 'Median Follow-Up Duration' in months for the participants.

Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 1 states: 'Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel.' The 'Trial Design' section on page 2 further specifies: 'Enrolled patients were randomly assigned (1:1) to oral darolutamide 600 mg twice daily or matched placebo, in combination with ADT and docetaxel.' Figure 1 also labels one arm as 'Darolutamide + docetaxel + ADT'.

NCT.evidence: Found under 'CLINICAL TRIAL INFORMATION' on page 12.

PubMed ID.evidence: PubMed ID is not explicitly reported in the document.

Trial Name.value: Darolutamide Plus Androgen-Deprivation Therapy and Docetaxel in Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer by Disease Volume and Risk Subgroups in the Phase III ARASENS Trial
Trial Name.evidence: Extracted from the main title of the article on page 1.

Author.evidence: Found the author list on page 1, starting with 'Maha Hussain, MD¹; Bertrand Tombal, MD, PhD2; Fred Saad, MD³; Karim Fizazi, MD, PhD4; Cora N. Sternberg, MD5;' and ending with 'and Matthew R. Smith, MD, PhD19'. Using 'et al.' for brevity as requested.

Year.evidence: Found 'J Clin Oncol 00.© 2023 by American Society of Clinical Oncology' on page 1 and 'Copyright© 2023 American Society of Clinical Oncology. All rights reserved.' on multiple pages.

Full Pub or Abstract.evidence: The document is extensive, spanning multiple pages with detailed methods, results, tables, and figures, indicating it is a full publication rather than just an abstract. The 'abstract' section is clearly labeled as a part of the full document.

Phase.evidence: Found 'Phase III ARASENS Trial' in the title on page 1 and 'ARASENS was a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, phase III trial' under 'Trial Design' in the Methods section on page 2.

Original/Follow Up.evidence: The title and abstract describe the study as presenting efficacy and safety from the ARASENS trial for subgroups, and the 'INTRODUCTION' section refers to 'early treatment intensification' and 'evaluated in several phase III trials (PEACE-1, ENZAMET, and ARASENS)'. The 'Original reports' label on page 1 also indicates this is an original report of findings from the ARASENS trial, not a follow-up of a previously published analysis of ARASENS.

Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.value: Androgen receptor pathway inhibitor (Darolutamide), Androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT), and Chemotherapy (Docetaxel)
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 1 states that patients were randomly assigned to 'darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel'. The 'INTRODUCTION' section on page 1 further clarifies that 'darolutamide' is an 'androgen receptor pathway inhibitor' and 'docetaxel' is a chemotherapy agent. ADT is androgen-deprivation therapy, a form of hormonal therapy.
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.group_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1

Quality of Life reported.evidence: On page 3, under 'Study Assessments', it states: 'Secondary efficacy end points included time to pain progression on the basis of the Brief Pain Inventory Short-Form questionnaire' and 'time to worsening of disease-related physical symptoms as measured by the Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Prostate (National Comprehensive Cancer Network) symptom index 17-item questionnaire'. The 'DISCUSSION' section on page 6 also mentions: 'Time to pain progression and symptomatic skeletal events showed improvement with HRs below one across all volume and risk subgroups'.

Trial.evidence: The name of the clinical study, 'ARASENS Trial', is explicitly mentioned in the title of the document: 'Darolutamide Plus Androgen-Deprivation Therapy and Docetaxel in Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer by Disease Volume and Risk Subgroups in the Phase III ARASENS Trial'. It is also frequently referenced throughout the text, for example, in the abstract and introduction.

Median Age (years) | Treatment.evidence: On page 5, in 'TABLE 1. Baseline Demographics and Patient Characteristics for the Subgroups of Patients by Disease Volume and Disease Risk in ARASENS', under 'Characteristic at Baseline', the 'Age, median, years (range)' for 'Darolutamide (n = 497)' (treatment arm) is '67.0 (41-89)'. The median age is 67.0 years.

Median Age (years) | Control.evidence: On page 5, in 'TABLE 1. Baseline Demographics and Patient Characteristics for the Subgroups of Patients by Disease Volume and Disease Risk in ARASENS', under 'Characteristic at Baseline', the 'Age, median, years (range)' for 'Placebo (n = 508)' (control arm) is '67.0 (44-86)'. The median age is 67.0 years.

Quality of Life Scale.value: Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Prostate (National Comprehensive Cancer Network) symptom index 17-item questionnaire
Quality of Life Scale.evidence: On page 3, under the 'Study Assessments' section, it states: 'time to worsening of disease-related physical symptoms as measured by the Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Prostate (National Comprehensive Cancer Network) symptom index 17-item questionnaire'. This questionnaire assesses disease-related physical symptoms, which is a measure of participants' overall well-being and satisfaction with their health status, reflecting their perceived quality of life.

Control Arm - N.evidence: The CONSORT flow diagram (Figure 1) on page 3 shows that 655 patients were randomly assigned to the 'Placebo + docetaxel + ADT' arm. One patient was 'Excluded for GCP violation', resulting in 654 patients 'Analyzed for primary end point'. Table 1 on page 5, under 'All Patients (N = 1,305)', also reports 'Placebo (n = 654)' as the number of participants in the control arm for baseline characteristics and primary analysis.

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Trea.evidence: Found in the first paragraph of page 11: "median treatment duration in ARASENS was 41 months for darolutamide plus ADT and docetaxel".

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Cont.evidence: The document provides median on-treatment duration for the control arm only for specific subgroups (e.g., 15.3 months for high-volume, 26.5 months for low-volume on page 4), but an overall median duration for the control arm is not reported.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | S.evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 2 states: 'The presence of metastatic disease at initial diagnosis (de novo or synchronous disease) is associated with poorer outcomes compared with developing metastases after local therapy (recurrent or metachronous disease).' The 'RESULTS' section on page 2 further reports: 'In ARASENS, the treatment effect of darolutamide on OS was favorable in patients with de novo (HR, 0.71; 95% CI, 0.59 to 0.85) and recurrent (HR, 0.61; 95% CI, 0.35 to 1.05) metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer.' 'De novo' is synonymous with synchronous disease. Additionally, Table 1 on page 5, under 'Metastasis stage at initial diagnosis, No. (%)', includes 'De novo mHSPC' as a subgroup for which data is reported.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | M.evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 2 states: 'The presence of metastatic disease at initial diagnosis (de novo or synchronous disease) is associated with poorer outcomes compared with developing metastases after local therapy (recurrent or metachronous disease).' The 'RESULTS' section on page 2 further reports: 'In ARASENS, the treatment effect of darolutamide on OS was favorable in patients with de novo (HR, 0.71; 95% CI, 0.59 to 0.85) and recurrent (HR, 0.61; 95% CI, 0.35 to 1.05) metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer.' 'Recurrent' is synonymous with metachronous disease in this context. Additionally, Table 1 on page 5, under 'Metastasis stage at initial diagnosis, No. (%)', includes 'Recurrent mHSPC' as a subgroup for which data is reported.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | H.evidence: The title of the paper on page 1 is 'Darolutamide Plus Androgen-Deprivation Therapy and Docetaxel in Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer by Disease Volume and Risk Subgroups in the Phase III ARASENS Trial'. The 'PURPOSE' section on page 1 states: 'We examined efficacy and safety from the ARASENS trial for subgroups by disease volume and risk.' The 'RESULTS' section on page 1 explicitly states: 'Darolutamide increased overall survival (OS) versus placebo in patients with high-volume (hazard ratio [HR], 0.69; 95% CI, 0.57 to 0.82)...'. Table 1 on page 5 is titled 'Baseline Demographics and Patient Characteristics for the Subgroups of Patients by Disease Volume and Disease Risk in ARASENS' and includes a 'High Volume' column. Figure 2 on page 6 also presents 'OS in subgroups of patients by (A) high-volume disease'.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | L.evidence: The title of the paper on page 1 is 'Darolutamide Plus Androgen-Deprivation Therapy and Docetaxel in Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer by Disease Volume and Risk Subgroups in the Phase III ARASENS Trial'. The 'PURPOSE' section on page 1 states: 'We examined efficacy and safety from the ARASENS trial for subgroups by disease volume and risk.' The 'RESULTS' section on page 1 explicitly states: '...and in the smaller low-volume subgroup, the results were also suggestive of survival benefit (HR, 0.68; 95% CI, 0.41 to 1.13).' Table 1 on page 5 is titled 'Baseline Demographics and Patient Characteristics for the Subgroups of Patients by Disease Volume and Disease Risk in ARASENS' and includes a 'Low Volume' column. Figure 2 on page 6 also presents 'OS in subgroups of patients by (B) low-volume disease'.

Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Treat.evidence: Found in Table 1, under the 'High Volumea' section, the 'Darolutamide (n = 497)' column header indicates 497 patients. The total number of patients in the Darolutamide arm is 651 (497 high volume + 154 low volume from Table 1 headers). The percentage is calculated as (497 / 651) * 100 = 76.3%.

Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Contr.evidence: Found in Table 1, under the 'High Volumea' section, the 'Placebo (n = 508)' column header indicates 508 patients. The total number of patients in the Placebo arm is 654 (508 high volume + 146 low volume from Table 1 headers). The percentage is calculated as (508 / 654) * 100 = 77.7%.

Volume of disease - N (%) | Low | Contro.evidence: Found in Table 1, under the 'Low Volumea' section, the 'Placebo (n = 146)' column header indicates 146 patients. The total number of patients in the Placebo arm is 654 (508 high volume + 146 low volume from Table 1 headers). The percentage is calculated as (146 / 654) * 100 = 22.3%.

Primary Endpoint(s).evidence: On page 3, under the 'Study Assessments' section, the document explicitly states: 'OS was the primary end point.'

Secondary Endpoint(s).value: time to castration-resistant prostate cancer, time to pain progression on the basis of the Brief Pain Inventory Short-Form questionnaire, symptomatic skeletal event-free survival, time to first symptomatic-skeletal event, time to initiation of subsequent systemic antineoplastic therapy, time to worsening of disease-related physical symptoms as measured by the Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Prostate (National Comprehensive Cancer Network) symptom index 17-item questionnaire, and time to initiation of opioid use for 7 or more days
Secondary Endpoint(s).evidence: On page 3, under the 'Study Assessments' section, it states: 'OS was the primary end point. Secondary efficacy end points included time to castration-resistant prostate cancer, time to pain progression on the basis of the Brief Pain Inventory Short-Form questionnaire, symptomatic skeletal event-free survival, time to first symptomatic-skeletal event, time to initiation of subsequent systemic antineoplastic therapy, time to worsening of disease-related physical symptoms as measured by the Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Prostate (National Comprehensive Cancer Network) symptom index 17-item questionnaire, and time to initiation of opioid use for 7 or more days.'

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Treat.evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 1 states that patients were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel. Figure 1, 'CONSORT flow diagram' on page 3, shows that for the 'Darolutamide + docetaxel + ADT' arm, 'Patients randomly assigned (n = 651)' and 'Received allocated treatment (n = 651)'. Since docetaxel was part of the allocated treatment, 651 out of 651 patients in this arm received docetaxel, which is 100%.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Contr.evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 1 states that patients were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel. Figure 1, 'CONSORT flow diagram' on page 3, shows that for the 'Placebo + docetaxel + ADT' arm, 'Patients randomly assigned (n = 655)' and 'Received allocated treatment (n = 651)'. Since docetaxel was part of the allocated treatment, 651 out of 655 patients in this arm received docetaxel, which is (651/655) * 100% = 99.39%, rounded to 99.4%.

Total Participants - N.evidence: The CONSORT flow diagram (Figure 1, page 3) explicitly states the number of patients who 'Received allocated treatment'. For the Darolutamide + docetaxel + ADT arm, this was n = 651. For the Placebo + docetaxel + ADT arm, this was also n = 651. Summing these two groups (651 + 651) yields a total of 1302 participants who were included in either the treatment or control arm of the clinical trial. This number is further supported by the 'All Patients' total (N=1302) in Table 3 (page 9) and Table 4 (page 10).

COE_RCT_IND_OVERALL_RJ.evidence: The document is a report of a single randomized controlled trial (ARASENS trial) and its subgroup analyses. It does not provide an 'overall risk judgment of individual criteria of evidence (COE) in randomized controlled trials (RCT)' for other studies, which would typically be found in a systematic review or meta-analysis assessing the quality of multiple RCTs.

Add-on Treatment.evidence: The title of the paper is "Darolutamide Plus Androgen-Deprivation Therapy and Docetaxel in Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer...". The Methods section on page 1 states: "Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel." This indicates that darolutamide is the additional therapy given in combination with androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) and docetaxel.

Median OS (mo) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: Found in Figure A1, page 16, in the 'Overall' row under the 'Darolutamide + ADT + Docetaxel' column, where the median OS is reported as 'NE' (Not Estimable).

Median OS (mo) | Overall | Control.evidence: Found in Figure A1, page 16, in the 'Overall' row under the 'Placebo + ADT + Docetaxel' column, where the median OS is reported as '42.4'.

Median OS (mo) | High volume | Treatment.evidence: Found in Figure 2A, page 6, which displays OS in patients with high-volume disease. The Darolutamide + ADT + docetaxel arm shows 'Median, NE (95% CI, 50.3 months to NE)'.

Median OS (mo) | High volume | Control.evidence: Found in Figure 2A, page 6, which displays OS in patients with high-volume disease. The Placebo + ADT + docetaxel arm shows 'Median, 42.4 months (95% CI, 39.7 to 46.0)'.

Median OS (mo) | Low volume | Treatment.evidence: Found in Figure 2B, page 6, which displays OS in patients with low-volume disease. The Darolutamide + ADT + docetaxel arm shows 'Median, NE (95% CI, NE to NE)'.

Median OS (mo) | Low volume | Control.evidence: Found in Figure 2B, page 6, which displays OS in patients with low-volume disease. The Placebo + ADT + docetaxel arm shows 'Median, NE (95% CI, NE to NE)'.

Type of Therapy.evidence: The METHODS section of the abstract states: "Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel." The CONCLUSION section also mentions "treatment intensification with darolutamide, androgen-deprivation therapy, and docetaxel." The 'Trial Design' subsection under METHODS on page 2 further clarifies: "Enrolled patients were randomly assigned (1:1) to oral darolutamide 600 mg twice daily or matched placebo, in combination with ADT and docetaxel." This indicates the active treatment arm received darolutamide, ADT, and docetaxel.

Treatment Class.value: Triplet therapy (Darolutamide, Androgen-Deprivation Therapy, and Docetaxel)
Treatment Class.evidence: The abstract states that patients were 'randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel'. The conclusion refers to 'treatment intensification with darolutamide, androgen-deprivation therapy, and docetaxel'. The 'Relevance' section on page 2 explicitly calls it 'Triplet therapy for predominantly de novo docetaxel fit metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer'.

Region - N (%) | North America | Treatme.evidence: Found in Figure A1, page 16, under "Geographical region", "North America" row, "Darolutamide + ADT + Docetaxel" column, "No. of Events/No. of Patients" section (95 patients for high-volume) and Figure A1, page 17, under "Geographical region", "North America" row, "Darolutamide + ADT + Docetaxel" column, "No. of Events/No. of Patients" section (30 patients for low-volume). Total = 95 + 30 = 125. Percentage calculated as (125 / 651 total Darolutamide patients) * 100 = 19.2%.

Region - N (%) | North America | Control.evidence: Found in Figure A1, page 16, under "Geographical region", "North America" row, "Placebo + ADT + Docetaxel" column, "No. of Events/No. of Patients" section (88 patients for high-volume) and Figure A1, page 17, under "Geographical region", "North America" row, "Placebo + ADT + Docetaxel" column, "No. of Events/No. of Patients" section (31 patients for low-volume). Total = 88 + 31 = 119. Percentage calculated as (119 / 654 total Placebo patients) * 100 = 18.2%.

PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: The document reports 'Time to CRPC' (a measure of progression-free survival) for the overall population in Table 2, with a median of 'NR' (Not Reached) for the Darolutamide arm and a Hazard Ratio of 0.36 (95% CI, 0.30 to 0.42) for Darolutamide vs Placebo. However, it does not provide a specific 'PFS Rate (%)' (percentage) for the overall treatment arm.

PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Control.evidence: The document reports 'Time to CRPC' (a measure of progression-free survival) for the overall population in Table 2, with a median of '19.1' months for the Placebo arm. However, it does not provide a specific 'PFS Rate (%)' (percentage) for the overall control arm. The Hazard Ratio of 0.36 (95% CI, 0.30 to 0.42) is a comparative measure for Darolutamide vs Placebo, not a value for the control arm in isolation.
